title: malapoenna xanthophylla
intro: Malapoenna xanthophylla, also known as the "yellow horned poppy," is a plant species that belongs to the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive horn-shaped seed pods. The malapoenna xanthophylla plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as protopine and berberine. In herbal medicine, the plant is used to treat various health conditions, including pain, fever, and respiratory issues, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and promoting relaxation. The malapoenna xanthophylla plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for those seeking natural relief.
